text: Token source of truth in the monorepo is packages/config/src/design.ts (single TS file) + apps/web/src/app/globals.css (manual mirror until v1.1 codegen). There is no apps/web/src/config/design.ts. Mobile tailwind is regenerated by 'pnpm gen:mobile-tw' from packages/config; never hand-edit apps/mobile/tailwind.config.js. Future plans touching design tokens must edit packages/config/src/design.ts and re-mirror globals.css in the same commit.

apps/mobile/app/(auth)/login.tsx and sign-up.tsx contain hardcoded 'Muscat' brand strings — leftover from a prior fork. Other mobile files may carry similar literals. When touching mobile code, grep for 'Muscat' (and other prior-fork display names) and route through brand.name instead. Worth an ESLint rule in mobile (currently only enforced on web) as a follow-up.

ESLint custom rule no-brand-string-literal at tooling/eslint-config/src/rules/no-brand-string-literal.mjs reads brand.name at lint time from a hardcoded candidate path list. Moving brand.ts requires updating that candidates list in the same commit, otherwise the rule silently no-ops (returns empty brandName).

apps/mobile/package.json does not currently depend on @mlabs/config workspace package (only @mlabs/validators). Future plans that need shared design or brand tokens in mobile must add @mlabs/config to mobile dependencies AND verify metro resolves workspace packages (no per-package metro config exists today).

apps/mobile/app.config.ts contains 'Muscat'/'muscat' literals (bundle identifier, deep-link host, scheme, photo-permission text) that LOOK like stale fork strings but are deliberate placeholders documented in the file itself. They're handled by 'pnpm rename' + FORK_CHECKLIST.md, not by brand-string sweeps. Don't auto-fix them when sweeping stale prior-fork strings.

WCAG contrast affects more than just text-on-bg pairs. The check-contrast script enforces 3:1 for border, input, and ring (focus indicator) vs background. When introducing a vivid primary color, the focus ring is the trickiest pair — orange #FF6B2C straddles the 3:1 line on white (2.98:1). Solution: darken the ring (and matching border/input) slightly while keeping primary itself at the brand value. Trade: ring becomes deep coral, not exact brand orange. Acceptable; brand still reads.

When moving brand.ts between packages, the ESLint rule no-brand-string-literal needs its candidate path list updated AND its error message text. The cwd at lint time is the per-package directory (e.g. apps/web), so candidate paths must include ../../packages/config/src/brand.ts as well as the absolute-from-root form. path.join normalizes the .. segments correctly.

shadcn Button from @mlabs/ui-web wraps @base-ui/react/button which does NOT support asChild. To render a Link styled as a button, import buttonVariants and use cn(buttonVariants({...})) directly on the Link's className. Avoid the <Button><Link/></Button> nesting pattern: it produces invalid HTML (a inside button) and breaks middle-click + open-in-new-tab behaviour.

Phase 9 review found 14 consumer sites for the legacy apps/web/src/features/{messages,notifications}/server/* modules (the TODO claimed 13). The @mlabs/services equivalents use a different contract: (db, ctx, args) signatures and wrapped return shapes ({ items }, { count }, { message }) instead of bare values. Two functions also renamed: listForUser -> listConversations, unreadCount -> getUnreadCount. The messages service test directory does not exist at all (TODO said empty); porting requires creating the directory.

TODO #32's file list misidentified apps/web/src/features/notifications/server/actions.ts as a legacy duplicate. In reality actions.ts had already been rewired during the monorepo migration to call operations (markReadOp, markAllReadOp), and the client components depend on it as a Server Action shim. The actual legacy files are create.ts + queries.ts only. Lesson: when a TODO lists files for deletion, read each file's imports before trusting the categorization.

@mlabs/services packages enforce no-restricted-imports in tooling/eslint-config — consumers cannot reach into @mlabs/services/messages/service or @mlabs/services/notifications/service. All imports go through the @mlabs/services root barrel (namespace-style: import { messages, notifications } from '@mlabs/services'). Tests within the service package can import from ./service directly; tests/consumers in other packages cannot.

When a service package uses 'export * as <namespace>' barrels, type-only imports cannot use 'import type { X } from "@mlabs/services"' — the type lives under the namespace, not top-level. Use the published subpath instead: 'import type { NotificationRow } from "@mlabs/services/notifications"'. Same applies to all namespace-style re-exports.

When moving a file in an atomic-commit run, transient broken imports in to-be-deleted files (e.g. tests scheduled for deletion in the very next commit) must still be patched in the move commit, not left for the delete commit — otherwise the move commit alone won't typecheck. The cost is one extra line of churn that gets removed in the next commit; the benefit is bisect-clean atomic commits.
